title: Extend detail-view children table to show full descendant tree

decisions.question: Where does the detail view get the deep descendant tree — a new call to the parent brief's writ-tree endpoint with rootId, or keep the existing writ-list?parentId fan-out and recurse client-side?
decisions.rationale: #3 fires cleanly — writ-tree with rootId is the existing API extension point; client-side recursion is routing around it.

decisions.question: Does the detail view still call writ-show (for parent context, links, summary) alongside writ-tree, or does writ-tree alone drive the detail render?
decisions.rationale: #15 fires against merging writ-tree's deep-descendants output into writ-show's contract — hold the hard line between the two tools' responsibilities.

decisions.question: What depth cap does the detail view use for its subtree render?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks to the specific cap value — confirming the primer.

decisions.question: What does the detail-view overflow row do when clicked?
decisions.rationale: #26 fires — the detail-view drill-in is the existing surface; inline-expand and non-interactive both invent new affordances without cause.

decisions.question: Does the detail view get per-node expand/collapse toggles, or does it always render the subtree fully expanded (subject to the cap)?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks to toggle vs always-expanded — no-toggle-roots-only is eliminated on its own terms; confirming the primer between the remaining two.

decisions.question: Is the detail view's collapsed-set shared with the main list's collapsed-set, or scoped to the detail view and reset on each open?
decisions.rationale: #18 fires — persistence and cross-view sharing both add structure without a named second consumer; reset is the earn-it-later default.

decisions.question: How is the depth-aware rendering logic organized between the main list and the detail view — extract a shared pure helper, or duplicate the depth/overflow/toggle logic inline in renderDetail()?
decisions.rationale: #15 fires — generalizing sortedFilteredWrits would merge sort/search concerns across the main-list/detail-view contract; a narrower shared helper keeps the boundary clean.

decisions.question: Does the shallow children-summary badge strip (above the children table) change — extend to a deep rollup, keep shallow, or remove?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires — this brief's named question is deep-descendants visibility, not summary-strip shape; obs-2 is explicitly separate scope.

decisions.question: Do the per-row Actions buttons (row-publish, row-cancel) and the row-click-to-open-detail handler apply to deep-descendant rows, or only to direct children?
decisions.rationale: No principle speaks to action-column scope — confirming the primer's main-list-parity call.

decisions.question: Column set of the detail-view children table — stays at the current narrow set (Phase | Title | Type | ID | Actions, no Created), or align with the main list (add Created)?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires — the brief did not name a column-set question; status quo is the thinnest slice.

decisions.question: Does the detail view gain a global Children toggle button (the main list's 'just show roots' shortcut), or stay without one?
decisions.rationale: #18 fires — a global toggle is new structure with no named consumer; per-node toggles already cover targeted collapse.

decisions.question: When the detail view's writ has no descendants at all (truly childless), does the Children section render an empty table, an empty-state message, or hide entirely as it does today?
decisions.rationale: #23 fires — empty-state UX is not the named question; status quo keeps scope disciplined.
